Nicolás Madoery · Culture · Internet · AI

Thinking about technology
from a human perspective.

I work on how artificial intelligence, emerging technologies and digital platforms are transforming organizations, communities and cultural systems.

I develop applied research, protocols and programs for organizations that need to understand the impact of AI, emerging technologies, digital platforms and new community models.

[01] / Method Research / Strategy / Implementation

Explore, translate, structure.

Three movements organize my work with organizations, teams and institutions going through technological transformation. Research, strategy and systems to understand, implement and structure the impact of artificial intelligence and digital platforms.

Cuaderno · Method en construcción
/01
Explore

I research tensions, risks and opportunities around artificial intelligence, digital platforms and organizational transformation. I analyze context before proposing solutions.

Diagnostics · Mapping · Signal detection · Benchmarking · Use cases
/02
Translate

I turn technological complexity into understandable frameworks for organizations, teams and communities. I design conversations and tools that help people make decisions with more clarity and judgment.

Decision frameworks · Workshops · Materials · Roadmaps · Training
/03
Structure

I design strategies, protocols and technology adoption systems for organizations that need to incorporate artificial intelligence without losing governance, traceability or human judgment.

AI protocols · Governance · Adoption systems · Programs · Implementation
Outputs
Applied research Diagnostics Protocolos IA Workshops Strategy Governance Training
[02] / About Buenos Aires

Research, operations and program design.

I come from music, digital media and Latin American public policy. That background shapes how I work: with sensitivity to context, a focus on how technologies reorganize ecosystems, and rigor in reading the tensions between communities, platforms and regulation.

I founded and lead FUTURX, a research and strategy studio focused on culture, the internet, artificial intelligence and emerging technologies. From there I develop research, training programs and consulting projects for organizations, institutions and teams going through technological transformation.

My work crosses artificial intelligence, digital platforms, emerging technologies and new community models. What connects these territories is a shared question: how do human practices reorganize when technical infrastructure changes?

I have participated in projects, programs and training initiatives with UNESCO, the British Council, ministries of culture across Latin America and international organizations connected to creativity, innovation and public policy.

I previously worked in record labels, digital platforms and public cultural management. That combination of operations, research and strategic design is the foundation of my work today.

I have also supported digital strategies and audience development for more than 200 cultural projects, including collaborations with Gustavo Santaolalla, Juana Molina, La Mona Jiménez and Florian, among others.

I co-create corporate training programs at Humano / Artificial with Gaba Najmanovich. I host the podcast Data a la Deriva.

I live in Buenos Aires. I work in Spanish and English.

[03] / Lines de trabajo 2026

Four fronts where I work today.

2020 → present Active

FUTURX

A research and strategy studio focused on artificial intelligence, the internet and cultural transformation. It develops research, training programs and content on how emerging technologies are reorganizing the cultural and creative sectors, with a focus on music.

Investigación Strategy Community
2024 → present Own framework

PIACH

A practical system for cultural, creative and institutional organizations to adopt AI with judgment. Use typologies, risk maps, decision criteria and operational protocols.

Protocols Governance Adoption
2025 → present Active

Data a la Deriva

An audiovisual podcast on AI, the internet and culture. Interviews with artists, researchers and operators shaping the relationship between technologies, platforms and cultural practices.

Podcast Video Conversation
2024 → present Active

Humano / Artificial

Co-created with Gaba Najmanovich. A space for training, exploration and support for organizations and teams that need to understand how artificial intelligence, the internet and new technologies are transforming work, communication and decision-making.

Training Trends Strategy Co-creation

Work together

[04] / Tensions Running through my work

Six tensions that run through my work.

/01

Culture remains a blind spot in much of the discussion about artificial intelligence.

/02

Digital platforms do not only distribute culture: they shape which cultures circulate.

/03

Automation makes human judgment more important, not less.

/04

Many organizations incorporate AI without clear frameworks for governance or quality.

/05

The discussion about AI is not only technological: it is political, economic and cultural.

/06

Digital communities and new decentralized organizational models are reconfiguring how culture is produced and distributed.
